lines: Morocco are absolutely minting it — a 3-0 demolition of Canada sends Butters, Callum, Jim, and Wilmot into the quarter-final draw together. That's a four-way payday, and Butters (1st, 72pts) is laughing his arse off at the top of the table.

France did what France do — ground out a 1-0 win over Paraguay, meaning Ellis watches her last surviving team get dumped out by the same smug French squad that's keeping Callum, Croft, Dene, and The Foreman alive. Ellis, you're down to Argentina and literally nothing else. Brilliant portfolio, you fuck.

Dene, let's be honest with ourselves — France is the only reason you're still in this sweepstake. Senegal? Gone. Bosnia? Gone. Tunisia never even made it out of the groups. You are basically a France fan now whether you like it or not. Humiliating.

Today's big one: Norway vs Brazil — Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all have a horse in that race. Al (9th, 38pts) needs Norway to survive just to stay relevant, while Butters is already sitting pretty and could pull further clear at the top. It's basically five mates screaming at the same team for entirely different reasons.

Then it's Mexico vs England — Carter and Wilmot both need the Three Lions to come through, and given England's historical ability to make even beating Mexico look like open-heart surgery, do not get comfortable. Watch out for Wilmot (4th, 59pts) bottling it in real time.
